items.q: How does a catalyst speed up a reaction?
items.correct: It provides an alternative reaction pathway with a lower activation energy.
items.misconception: A catalyst lowers the activation energy of the original reaction pathway.
items.random_wrong: A catalyst gives extra kinetic energy to the reactant particles.
items.why: Textbook diagrams often show 'Ea lowered' without making clear it's a new pathway, leading students to think catalysts modify the original pathway.

items.q: What happens to electrons when sodium reacts with chlorine?
items.correct: Sodium loses one electron to chlorine; the resulting Na+ and Cl- ions attract electrostatically.
items.misconception: Sodium and chlorine share an electron between them.
items.random_wrong: Protons move from chlorine to sodium to balance the charges.
items.why: Students over-apply the covalent (sharing) model to all bonding because sharing is introduced first and feels intuitive.

items.q: Where is almost all the mass of an atom located?
items.correct: In the nucleus, made up of protons and neutrons.
items.misconception: Electrons contribute significantly to the mass of an atom.
items.random_wrong: Mass is spread evenly throughout the entire atom including the electron shells.
items.why: Students see electrons listed alongside protons and neutrons and assume they contribute comparable mass — in reality an electron has ~1/1836 the mass of a proton.

items.correct: Gas particles colliding with container walls
items.why: It's Gas particles colliding with container walls. “Gravity on the gas” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What happens to gas pressure if you make the container smaller (same gas, same temperature)?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: It increases. “It decreases” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: If gas pressure is 100 kPa in a 2 L container, and the volume is halved to 1 L (same temperature), what is the new pressure in kPa?
items.why: The real figure is 200. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
